title: VIDA 1.0 direct binary release
description: Canonical root epic for delivering VIDA 1.0 through bounded implementation waves from Binary Foundation to Primary Switchover.

title: Wave 1 - Binary Foundation
description: Rust workspace, crate boundaries, minimal bootable vida binary, thin command shell, fast local proof loop, and temp-state harness seam.

title: Wave 1.1 - Minimal Rust workspace and bootable vida scaffold
description: Bring the staged Binary Foundation scaffold into the repository root as a Rust workspace with a bootable vida binary and fail-closed root command shell.

title: Wave 1.2 - Cheap local proof loop for Binary Foundation
description: Establish and prove canonical local build/test/help commands for Binary Foundation via Makefile and staged proof path.

title: Wave 1.3 - Fail-closed shell hardening
description: Cover unknown commands, bad boot arguments, and temp-state seam robustness without entering Wave 2 semantics.

title: Wave 2 - State And Migration Backbone
description: Authoritative state spine, compatibility classification, migration preflight/runner, bridge import-export, and migration tests.

title: Wave 3 - Instruction And Operator Surface Integration
description: Instruction loading/composition, precedence activation runtime, and minimal operator-visible surfaces for boot/status/doctor/memory.

title: Wave 4 - Task Execution On Selected Flows
description: First real vida task execution flows, route/receipt enforcement on selected flows, resumable run-path wiring, and selected-flow bridge replacement steps.

title: Wave 5 - Verification Visibility And Orchestration Hardening
description: Richer proofs, parity/conformance proving, trace/eval/operator visibility, and lawful adaptive orchestration payload integration.

title: Wave 6 - Primary Switchover
description: Measured cutover, rollback-safe switchover, and final promotion proofs for VIDA 1.0.

title: Agent-system payload integration across Waves A-F
description: Slice and integrate the autonomous role profiles and adaptive orchestration implementation plan as part of VIDA 1.0, not as a separate product.

title: Framework execution efficiency and autonomous follow-through hardening
description: Improve early TODO entry, plan-to-task routing, project-operations clarity, and define a canonical autonomous execution protocol for end-to-end delivery when plans/specs are already settled.

title: Define and implement tracked autonomous execution protocol improvements
description: Capture self-diagnosis findings as framework improvements, add protocol/instruction changes for automatic TODO entry and plan-to-task routing, and define an autonomous execution protocol for continuing settled plans/specs to completion.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: Autonomous execution protocol enabled and wired into framework instruction surfaces
